disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sharp heel pain that is most severe with the very first steps in the morning, eases with initial walking, then worsens again after prolonged standing
trigger_question_examples: When is the pain worst during the day?
What is it like when you first get out of bed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The first steps in the morning are agony, like stepping on a nail. It eases a bit once I get moving, but if I stand around for a while it flares up again.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain localised to the underside of the heel, towards the inner edge, sharp and stabbing in character
trigger_question_examples: Can you point to exactly where it hurts?
What does the pain feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's right on the bottom of my heel, more on the inside edge — a sharp, stabbing pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began gradually over two weeks and worsened after a change to flat, unsupportive footwear; no injury or trauma
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and was there any injury?
Did anything change before it got worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been niggling for a couple of weeks and got much worse after I started wearing some flat unsupportive shoes. I didn't twist it or fall or anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is markedly worse walking barefoot on hard surfaces
trigger_question_examples: Is it worse on any particular surface or in bare feet?
Does the flooring at home make a difference?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — walking barefoot on the kitchen tiles is the worst.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No numbness, tingling, or pain radiating up the leg
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness, pins and needles, or pain shooting up your leg?
Does the pain ever feel like burning or an electric tingle?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, none of that — no numbness or pins and needles, and it doesn't shoot up my leg.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pain at rest or at night waking from sleep
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain wake you at night or hurt when you are resting?
Is there any pain when you are off your feet, sitting or lying down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't wake me at night and it's fine when I'm sitting or lying down — it's the weight-bearing that sets it off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The heel is not hot, red, or swollen and there is no fever
trigger_question_examples: Is the heel hot, red or swollen? Any fevers?
Does the skin over the heel look or feel any different?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it looks normal — not hot or red or swollen, and I don't have a temperature.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to push the foot down and rise onto the toes, though it is uncomfortable; no sudden pop or giving way
trigger_question_examples: Can you push your foot down or stand on your tiptoes?
Did you feel any sudden pop or snap, or did the ankle give way?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can push my foot down and get up on my toes, it's just sore. There was no pop or snap and it didn't suddenly give way.
